title: DSPy Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using DSPy, focusing on API parameters, CLI flags, and prompt techniques.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the output, balancing creativity and determinism.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content that still needs to adhere to some structure.

commands.description: Selects the specific AI model version for processing requests.
commands.scenario: When needing a balance between performance and cost for general tasks.

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in the generated output.
commands.scenario: When generating concise responses to save on processing costs.

commands.description: Enables streaming mode for real-time output.
commands.scenario: When building applications that require immediate feedback, like chatbots.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require logical reasoning.

commands.description: Specifies the output format for easier parsing and integration.
commands.scenario: When integrating AI responses into systems that require structured data.

commands.description: Discourages repetition by penalizing frequent tokens.
commands.scenario: When generating diverse and varied text outputs.

commands.description: Encourages the inclusion of new topics in the output.
commands.scenario: When aiming for exploratory and broad-ranging content.

commands.description: Sets the logging level to debug for detailed output during development.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting or developing new features with DSPy.

commands.command: Use concise language and avoid jargon.
commands.description: Instructs the model to produce clear and simple outputs.
commands.scenario: When generating content for a broad audience with varying expertise levels.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for requests to prevent long-running processes.
commands.scenario: When operating in environments with strict time constraints.

commands.description: Uses nucleus sampling to limit the token selection to a subset with cumulative probability.
commands.scenario: When you want a balance between randomness and reliability in output.

commands.description: Automatically retries failed requests up to a specified number of times.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in environments with intermittent connectivity.

commands.description: Use the BootstrapFewShot optimizer to automatically generate high-quality few-shot demonstrations by bootstrapping successful traces from your training data.
Parallelize evaluation across your dataset using the num_threads parameter to significantly reduce the time required for metric scoring.
Debug the exact prompt sent to the LLM by inspecting the internal history of calls, allowing you to verify how signatures are being compiled.
Enhance prompt engineering by explicitly defining 'instructions' within a Signature to guide the model's reasoning behavior beyond just input/output fields.
Enforce strict schema adherence by wrapping predictors with Pydantic models, ensuring the LLM output is structurally valid for downstream code.
Sets the global language model instance for all subsequent DSPy modules and signatures in the session.
A specialized predictor that instructs the model to generate code or logical steps to solve a problem rather than just text.
An advanced optimizer that uses Bayesian optimization and Large Language Models to iteratively refine both instructions and few-shot examples.
Accesses the internal history of the language model object to debug exactly what prompts were sent and what completions were received.
Implements programmatic constraints within a DSPy module to trigger retries or error handling if a specific logical condition is not met.
